title: potentilla multifida subsp. arctica
intro: Potentilla multifida subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Europe. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Potentilla multifida subsp. arctica are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
